name: Pork Empanadas (Meat Turnovers)
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Pork Butt or shoulder roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pork in a large pot. Cover with water and add onion, garlic, 1/2 teaspoon salt, oregano, cumin, and bay leaves.
2. Bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
3. While the pork cooks, prepare the dough by sifting together flour, Masa Harina, baking powder, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a separate bowl. Beat egg and milk together separately.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the egg mixture. Stir with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
5. Divide the dough into 16 even pieces and roll each piece into a ball. Place in a bowl, cover, and refrigerate.
6. After pork has simmered for 1 1/2 hours, preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Remove pork from liquid, saving the stock if desired.
7. Place pork in a baking dish or Dutch oven and brush with hot pepper sauce. Bake for 1 hour, or until meat shreds easily with fork. Remove from oven and shred into small pieces.
8. Combine red Chile Salsa (see #141) and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt on a lightly floured surface.
9. Flatten a piece of dough with the palm of your hand. Roll out the dough to make a 5-inch circle, about 1/8 inch thick using a rolling pin or cut-off broom handle.
10. Place 2-3 tablespoons filling in the center of the dough. Brush edges of dough with milk. Fold dough over filling and pinch edges together. Crimp with a fork to seal.
11. Place empanada on a lightly greased baking sheet. Bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from oven and cool for about 5 minutes before serving warm.
12. Empanadas can be frozen baked or unbaked. If baking from the freezer, do not thaw; bake in the preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 25-30 minutes. Reheat baked frozen empanadas at 350°F (175°C) for 15 minutes.
